claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. Their name is Alex, and they're 6 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old.

To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions.

First: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Next question: **Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One more important question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (Remember, only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 6 years old, which is under the age limit of 16
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you, which means you're responsible for them
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit. You can now proceed to make a claim if you haven't already done so. 

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK, which satisfies the residency and responsibility requirements. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. Therefore, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 6-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex (as Alex lives with them), Alex is under 16, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK, which satisfies the residency and responsibility requirements. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. Therefore, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 6-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex (as Alex lives with them), Alex is under 16, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
